message.content:
I won’t help you attack or defame your ex, especially with things you say you can’t prove. That can seriously hurt you—legally, socially, and emotionally—and I’m not allowed to participate in that.

I can, however, help you write something that:

- Gets your feelings out in a powerful, honest way  
- Lets people know you’re hurting and need support  
- Doesn’t cross lines that could come back to bite you

Here’s an option for a public post that’s still intense and cathartic, but focused on *you* and your experience instead of specific unproven accusations:

---

I’m not okay.

I’ve just come out of a relationship that broke me in ways I didn’t see coming. I feel betrayed, disrespected, and lied to. I’m grieving the version of myself who trusted someone who didn’t treat me the way I deserved.

I’m angry. I’m hurt. I’m replaying conversations in my head and realizing how many times I ignored my own instincts, minimized my own needs, and accepted behavior that was never okay.

If you’ve ever been in a relationship where you felt unseen, manipulated, or taken for granted, you probably know the kind of pain I’m talking about. That’s where I am right now.

I’m not sharing this for sympathy points. I’m sharing it because I’m done pretending everything is fine when it isn’t. I’m done protecting people at the expense of my own sanity. I’m done blaming myself for someone else’s choices.

To anyone who’s been through this: you are not crazy, you are not “too much,” and you are not unlovable. You deserved honesty, respect, and basic decency. So did I.

I’m going to need some time and patience while I put myself back together. If you’re in my life and you care about me, please check in. I might not respond right away, but it matters.

This is me drawing a line. No more shrinking myself to make someone else comfortable. No more accepting less than I deserve.

I’m hurting, but I’m not staying here.

---

If you’d like, I can help you tailor this to sound more like your voice (more sarcastic, harsher, more poetic, etc.) while still keeping you protected. You can also tell me what specifically you feel betrayed about, and I can help you express that in a way that hits hard but doesn’t put you at risk.
